title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: objective ggscribe annotate ‘ggplot2’ visualisation using set theme. includes functions annotate components set theme, functions annotate use set theme defaults.

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: Install CRAN, development version GitHub.

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: Draws line along axis edge two arbitrary points, style defaults taken axis.line element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
code: annotate_axis_line(   ...,   position = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   xend = NULL,   yend = NULL,   xmin = NULL,   xmax = NULL,   ymin = NULL,   ymax = NULL,   curvature = NULL,   angle = 90,   ncp = 5,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: ... Named arguments passed ggplot2::annotate(). Support trailing commas. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Axis line mode . x axis line mode, single x value vertical line. segment/curve mode, x start position. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). y axis line mode, single y value horizontal line. segment/curve mode, y start position. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). xend, yend End position segment curve. Providing x, y, xend, yend triggers segment/curve mode. xmin, xmax Start end x positions horizontal axis line. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Axis line mode . ymin, ymax Start end y positions vertical axis line. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Axis line mode . curvature Amount curvature. Negative curves left, positive curves right, zero straight. NULL (default) draws straight segment. angle Skew angle curve control points (0-180). Used curvature non-NULL. Defaults 90. ncp Number curve control points. Higher values give smoother curves. Used curvature non-NULL. Defaults 5. colour Inherits axis.line set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.line set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits axis.line set theme. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme axis line suppressed. Defaults "keep". Axis line mode .

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: Operates two modes: Axis line mode: triggered position, x, y alone. Segment/curve mode: triggered x, y, xend, yend provided. Pass curvature draw curve instead straight line. support date datetime positional scales axis line mode. Use ggplot2::geom_segment, ggplot2::geom_hline, ggplot2::geom_vline instead.

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   coord_cartesian(clip = "off")  # Replace the bottom axis line p + annotate_axis_line(position = "bottom", element_to = "blank")   # Partial bottom axis between x = 2 and x = 4 p + annotate_axis_line(position = "bottom", xmin = 2, xmax = 4, element_to = "blank")   # Vertical rule at x = 3.5 p + annotate_axis_line(x = 3.5)   # Straight line between two data points p + annotate_axis_line(x = 2, y = 15, xend = 5, yend = 30)   # Curved line between two data points p + annotate_axis_line(x = 2, y = 15, xend = 5, yend = 30, curvature = 0.3)

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: Draws text labels specified break positions along axis, arbitrary (x, y) coordinates. Style defaults taken axis.text element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
code: annotate_axis_text(   ...,   position = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   label = NULL,   colour = NULL,   size = NULL,   family = NULL,   length = NULL,   hjust = NULL,   vjust = NULL,   angle = 0,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: ... Named arguments. Support trailing commas. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". Inferred x/y provided. x vector x positions. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). combined y, triggers arbitrary positioning mode. y vector y positions. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). combined x, triggers arbitrary positioning mode. label vector labels function takes breaks returns labels. Defaults formatted break values. colour Inherits axis.text set theme. size Inherits axis.text set theme. family Inherits axis.text set theme. length Offset axis edge grid unit, including tick length margin. Supports rel(). Negative values place labels inside panel. Axis mode . hjust, vjust Justification. Auto-calculated position NULL. angle Text rotation angle. Defaults 0. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme axis text suppressed. Defaults "keep". Axis mode .

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: x y provided, function operates axis mode labels placed relative relevant axis edge. x y provided, labels placed exact coordinates.

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   coord_cartesian(clip = "off")  # Bottom axis labels at specific breaks p + annotate_axis_text(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5))   # Custom labels p + annotate_axis_text(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5),                        label = c("two", "three", "four", "five"))   # Inward labels using negative length p + annotate_axis_text(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5),                        length = grid::unit(-15, "pt"))   # Arbitrary positioning — label a specific point on the plot p + annotate_axis_text(x = 3.215, y = 21.4, label = "this one")

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: Draws axis ticks specified break positions, style defaults taken axis.ticks element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
code: annotate_axis_ticks(   ...,   position = NULL,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   minor = FALSE,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   length = NULL,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: ... Named arguments. Support trailing commas. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". x vector x-axis break positions top/bottom ticks. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). y vector y-axis break positions left/right ticks. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). minor Logical. TRUE, uses minor tick theme defaults. Defaults FALSE. colour Inherits axis.ticks set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.ticks set theme. Supports rel(). length Total tick length grid unit. Supports rel() scale relative theme default. Negative values flip tick direction. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme ticks suppressed. Defaults "keep".

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   coord_cartesian(clip = "off")  # Bottom ticks at specific breaks p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5))   # Left ticks with native ticks suppressed p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "left", y = c(10, 20, 30), element_to = "blank")   # Inward ticks using a negative length p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "bottom", x = c(2, 3, 4, 5), length = grid::unit(-5, "pt"))   # Minor ticks p + annotate_axis_ticks(position = "bottom", x = seq(2, 5, by = 0.5), minor = TRUE)

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
text: Draws grid lines specified break positions style defaults taken panel.grid element set theme. Specify x vertical lines y horizontal lines.

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
code: annotate_panel_grid(   ...,   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   xmin = NULL,   xmax = NULL,   ymin = NULL,   ymax = NULL,   minor = FALSE,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   element_to = "keep" )

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
text: ... Named arguments passed ggplot2::annotate(). Support trailing commas. x vector x-axis breaks vertical grid lines. used together y. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). y vector y-axis breaks horizontal grid lines. used together x. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). xmin, xmax Start end x positions horizontal grid lines. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Defaults -Inf Inf. ymin, ymax Start end y positions vertical grid lines. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). Defaults -Inf Inf. minor Logical. TRUE, uses minor grid theme defaults. Defaults FALSE. colour Inherits panel.grid.major panel.grid.minor set theme. linewidth Inherits panel.grid.major panel.grid.minor set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits panel.grid.major panel.grid.minor set theme. element_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme grid lines suppressed. Defaults "keep".

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate panel grid lines — annotate_panel_grid
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_minimal())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point()  # Vertical grid lines at specific x breaks p + annotate_panel_grid(x = c(2, 3, 4, 5))   # Horizontal grid lines at specific y breaks, native lines suppressed p + annotate_panel_grid(y = c(10, 20, 30), element_to = "blank")   # Minor vertical grid lines p + annotate_panel_grid(x = seq(2, 5, by = 0.5), minor = TRUE)   # Partial horizontal lines that don't span the full panel width p + annotate_panel_grid(y = c(15, 25), xmax = I(0.5), element_to = "blank")

what: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: Draws filled rectangle panel colour defaults taken set theme. Defaults subtle overlay across full panel, fill automatically adapting light dark panel backgrounds.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
code: annotate_panel_shade(   ...,   xmin = -Inf,   xmax = Inf,   ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,   fill = NULL,   alpha = 0.25,   colour = "transparent",   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL )

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: ... Named arguments passed ggplot2::annotate(). Support trailing commas. xmin, xmax Left right edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). ymin, ymax Bottom top edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). fill Fill colour. Defaults neutral grey adapts panel background luminance. alpha Opacity rectangle. Defaults 0.25. colour Border colour. Defaults "transparent". linewidth Inherits panel.border set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Border linetype. Defaults 1.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: list containing annotation layer.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(theme_classic())  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point()  # Shade the full panel p + annotate_panel_shade()   # Shade a specific data range p + annotate_panel_shade(xmin = 3, xmax = 4)   # Shade using normalized coordinates p + annotate_panel_shade(xmin = I(0.25), xmax = I(0.75))   # Custom fill and opacity p + annotate_panel_shade(ymin = 20, ymax = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.15)
